Tests the length property of the arguments object in a JavaScript function.

# Purpose
This code defines a function `f_arg` that takes two parameters, `x` and `y`, and returns the `arguments` object, which contains all the arguments passed to the function. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that calling `f_arg` with three arguments results in an `arguments` object with a length of 3. This demonstrates that the `arguments` object captures all arguments passed to the function, not just those explicitly defined in the function's parameter list. The code serves as a simple test to illustrate the behavior of the `arguments` object in JavaScript.
